brief_summary: Cross-section diagram of a stabilizer pair mounted to a PCB baseline. Two housing bodies with stems in bores, joined by a horizontal wire. Four numbered coral callouts identify the lubrication contact points: ① wire barrel, ② stem bore, ③ wire hook-in-slot, ④ housing feet — the feet are emphasized as the commonly skipped step. Each callout names the lube (dielectric grease vs GPL-205 G0) and the amount.
data_sources: stabilizer-servicing-guide.mdx § 'Why stabilizers rattle' — three sources: wire-into-barrel, stem-in-bore, wire-hook-in-stem-slot
stabilizer-servicing-guide.mdx § 'The step most builders skip' — fourth contact point: housing feet against PCB
stabilizer-servicing-guide.mdx § 'Lube selection' table — dielectric grease for barrel/hook/feet, GPL-205 G0 for stem bore and stem cylinder
